footer.blurb: Independent web studio building premium websites and custom web systems. One workspace from scope to launch.

about.metaDescription: Learn about Komlan Kouhiko, founder of CrecyStudio — the independent web studio building premium websites and custom systems.

proof.practitioner: Practitioner on every project — no juniors, no handoffs

wontTake.label: The work I turn down
wontTake.title: What CrecyStudio won't take on.
wontTake.intro: Selectivity is how I keep quality high on every project. These aren't limitations — they're design choices.
wontTake.0: Projects that need a team of 5+ people — I'm a solo practitioner, not an agency, and I won't pretend otherwise.
wontTake.1: Clients who want daily check-ins or aren't comfortable with async-first communication.
wontTake.2: Tight deadlines paired with an undefined scope — I'd rather delay the start than rush a bad outcome.
wontTake.3: Design-only work with no involvement in implementation — the value I provide is strategy and build, not deliverable handoff.
wontTake.4: Projects where the brief changes significantly after scope is agreed — I hold to the scope so you get the price I quoted.

start.metaTitle: Book a free discovery call | CrecyStudio
start.metaDescription: Book a free 20-minute discovery call with Komlan. Tell me what you're building — no pitch, no commitment.
start.heroTitle: Let's talk about what you're building.
start.heroSub: No pitch deck, no sales script. A direct conversation about your project — scope, feasibility, and whether we're a fit.
start.step1Body: Name, email, and a few lines about your project. Takes under two minutes.
start.step2Title: I review and reply within 24 hours
start.step2Body: I'll confirm a time or propose two options. No auto-scheduling bots.
start.step3Body: I ask sharp questions. You get honest feedback on scope, timeline, and price range — then we decide together.
start.anchor1Label: The discovery call — no credit card, no commitment
start.anchor3Label: Web apps, portals, and automations — scoped together on the call
start.riskReversal: If we're not a fit after the call, I'll point you toward who is. No hard sell.
start.availabilityPlaceholder: e.g. Tue/Thu afternoons ET, or most weekday mornings
start.slotPickerNoSlots: No times available — try another day.
start.slotRequired: Please pick a date and time.
start.successBodyScheduled: Your call is booked. A Google Calendar invite is on its way to your inbox.
start.errorInvalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
start.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again or email hello@crecystudio.com.
start.successBody: I'll be in touch within 24 hours to confirm a time. A copy of your request is on its way to your inbox.
start.successDetail: In the meantime, take a look at how the studio works.

home.metaTitle: CrecyStudio | Websites and custom web systems
home.metaDescription: Independent web studio building premium websites and custom web systems — from polished marketing sites to client portals, booking flows, and custom dashboards. One workspace from scope to launch.
home.heroTitle: Built for where you’re going, not where you are.
home.heroSub: From a polished website to a full custom system — built by a practitioner who has shipped both.

whatWeBuild.label: What we build
whatWeBuild.title: From a polished website to the system that runs the business.

portals.title: Client portals
portals.body: A private workspace your customers actually use.

automation.title: Workflow automation

website.situation: I need a professional website for my business

app.situation: I have a software idea or internal tool to build

portal.situation: I manage client projects and need a real client system

rescue.situation: My site is broken, slow, or leaking leads

ecommerce.situation: I need an online store or help running the one I have

ventures.label: Studio ventures
ventures.title: Real systems we’ve shipped.
ventures.intro: Two SaaS products built and operated by the studio — not client testimonials. They prove the engineering capability for the Web Apps lane.

korent.name: Korent
korent.tagline: Management platform for inflatable rental businesses
korent.detail: Multi-tenant SaaS — booking, fleet tracking, delivery routing, and AI scheduling assist.

proveo.name: Proveo
proveo.tagline: Photo proof + CRM for service contractors
proveo.detail: AI photo detection, freemium billing, CRM pipeline, automated lifecycle emails.

diff.label: The difference
diff.title: Your project lives in a workspace.
diff.lede: Most studios disappear between invoice and launch. We built a client workspace so you can track milestones, upload assets, review the live preview, and leave feedback in one place.

how.label: Process
how.title: Three steps. No guesswork.
how.step1Title: Tell us what the website needs to do
how.step1Body: You send the essentials: business, offer, pages, goals, and urgency. We scope against reality instead of guessing from a sales call.
how.step2Title: Track the whole project in one workspace
how.step2Body: You review milestones, previews, files, and next steps in one place. No scattered email chain and no wondering what happens next.
how.step3Body: We connect the domain, test the forms, and hand over the finished system. Your code, content, and accounts stay yours.

founder.label: / Who you’re working with
founder.name: Komlan Kouhiko
founder.bio1: I didn’t come up through an agency or a computer science program. In 2024, I launched a car rental business in Northern Virginia with 7 cars and a commercial insurance policy. Turo was taking a significant cut of every booking, so I taught myself to build my own platform — a full booking and fleet management system — and kept the profits. I ran that business for over a year, managing operations, customers, and the codebase simultaneously.
founder.bio2: When operational costs pushed me to pivot, I posted on Facebook Marketplace offering to build websites. Got a few clients. They liked the work. Since then I’ve shipped operating platforms for a repair center, a dropshipping store, IT services, and fleet management — and two SaaS products that are live today.
founder.bio3: Every project at CrecyStudio comes through me. No juniors, no handoffs. You get a builder who has sat on your side of the table.

also.label: Also offered
also.systemsBody: Automation for teams buried in spreadsheet ops, repetitive handoffs, and manual client follow-up.
also.ecomBody: Storefront, checkout, and ops cleanup for online shops leaking time or conversions.

pricing.metaDescription: Clear pricing ranges before the work starts. Fixed estimates scoped to your project, with no hostage retainers or surprise add-ons.
pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.title: Clear ranges before the work starts.
pricing.intro: All prices are USD. We scope first, send a written estimate, and keep the number tied to the actual project — no hostage retainers, no surprise add-ons.

websitesSection.sectionTitle: Most website projects land in one of these bands.

starter.label: / Starter
starter.detail: For a clean, credible single-page site that needs to do one job well.

growth.label: / Growth
growth.detail: For most service businesses that need a multi-page site and a stronger first impression.

premium.label: / Premium
premium.detail: For businesses that need content depth, integrations, and more architectural lift.

webAppsSection.sectionTitle: Dashboards, portals, internal tools, and MVPs.
webAppsSection.note: All web app projects start with a strategy call. Ranges only — scope and complexity drive the final number.

discovery.label: / Discovery sprint
discovery.detail: 1–2 week paid scoping engagement for projects that need definition before an MVP commit.
discovery.meta: 1–2 weeks / written brief / shapes the MVP

mvp.label: / MVP
mvp.detail: Multi-role web application with auth, a custom data model, and working integrations.
mvp.meta: Custom auth / data model / integrations / launch-ready

standard.label: / Standard
standard.detail: Production-grade application with complex workflows, multi-tenant support, or significant data architecture.

custom.label: / Custom
custom.detail: Large-scale or multi-system builds where scope is determined in discovery.

automationSection.sectionTitle: Stop doing the same task twice.

quickFix.label: / Quick fix
quickFix.detail: One broken or manual process diagnosed and automated.
quickFix.meta: Single workflow / documented / owned by you

opsSystem.label: / Ops system
opsSystem.detail: Two to four connected workflows with documentation and a clean handoff.

systemsPartner.label: / Systems partner
systemsPartner.detail: Ongoing automation maintenance and iterative improvements to your ops stack.

build.label: / Build
build.detail: New store or full rebuild — scoped after intake based on platform, products, and conversion goals.

run.label: / Run
run.detail: Monthly ops: product updates, order flow monitoring, analytics, and platform maintenance.
run.meta: Monthly retainer / ops + analytics / platform care

fix.label: / Fix
fix.detail: Targeted repair — broken checkout, mobile layout issues, platform migration, or conversion audit.
fix.meta: Single fix / scoped upfront / fast turnaround

rescueSection.sectionTitle: Fix what you have without a full rebuild.

audit.label: / Rescue audit
audit.detail: 3–5 day diagnostic with a written report and prioritized fix list — whether or not you engage us for the sprint.

sprint.label: / Rescue sprint
sprint.detail: Audit plus a 1–2 week implementation of the highest-impact fixes. Audit fee applies to the sprint.
sprint.meta: Audit included / 1–2 week sprint / highest-impact fixes

careSection.sectionTitle: Your site stays healthy after launch.
careSection.note: Monthly plans billed in USD. 3-month minimum commitment, month-to-month after.

care.label: / Care
care.detail: Monthly health check, monitoring, content updates, plugin updates, and end-of-month email summary.

carePlus.label: / Care+
carePlus.detail: Care plus small feature work, analytics review, portal activity dashboard, and shorter response windows.

carePro.label: / Care Pro
carePro.detail: Care+ plus a monthly improvement sprint, a 30-minute strategy call, and priority-response SLA.
carePro.meta: 8h bank + sprint / 4h ack / 1d resolve / strategy call

process.metaDescription: A simple process with clear milestones. Same execution rhythm for websites, e-commerce, and systems work so the scope stays visible.
process.label: Process
process.title: A simple process with clear milestones.
process.intro: Every lane uses the same execution rhythm so the scope stays visible, the pricing stays understandable, and nobody wonders what happens next.
process.lead: The point is not to make the process feel fancy. The point is to make it hard for work to disappear into silence.

step1.title: Tell us what the business needs
step1.body: You submit the lane-specific intake — goals, constraints, examples, and urgency. Komlan reviews it personally and comes back within 24 hours with questions or a scope draft. No sales call required to get started.

step2.title: Get a real estimate
step2.body: Komlan sends a written scope: pages or features, fixed price range, timeline, and assumptions. Nothing starts until you've reviewed and approved it in writing — no verbal agreements.

step3.title: Review everything in the workspace
step3.body: Work runs through your private workspace. You see every milestone as it ships, submit feedback in one place, and approve before anything goes live. Komlan is the only person building — no subcontractors, no handoffs.

step4.title: Go live with a clean handoff
step4.body: QA, domain setup, analytics, and a documented handoff of everything you need to maintain the work. You own all the files. If you want ongoing support, a care plan is available — but there's no pressure or lock-in.

process.founderNote: Every project at CrecyStudio runs through Komlan Kouhiko — not a project manager, not a junior developer. When you submit an intake, Komlan reviews it. When you have a question, Komlan answers it. When the work ships, Komlan built it.
process.byLaneTitle: The rhythm stays the same, the scope changes.

websites.title: Website builds
websites.body: Most projects land in the 2-4 week range depending on page count, copy readiness, and revision speed.

ecommerce.title: E-commerce work
ecommerce.body: Store builds, fixes, and ops retainers use the same rhythm, but the scope shifts based on storefront complexity and operational load.

systems.title: Systems work
systems.body: Automation projects always begin with diagnosis first so we solve the bottleneck instead of automating a broken process.

faq.metaDescription: Clear answers on scope, ownership, timelines, and how the project workspace works before anything is signed.
faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Answers before you commit.
faq.intro: Clear answers on scope, ownership, timelines, and how the project workspace works before anything is signed.
faq.sectionTitle: What people usually ask before signing.

estimate.q: How fast do I get an estimate?
estimate.a: Most website estimates go out within 24 hours of the intake. Larger e-commerce or systems work may need a quick follow-up before we lock scope.

scope.a: Websites are the main lane. We also handle e-commerce fixes and workflow automation when the business problem lives there instead.

ownership.q: Will I own the finished project?
ownership.a: Yes. Once the project is paid, you keep the code, content, domain access, and production accounts tied to the work.

portal.q: How does the client portal work?
portal.a: Clients get a workspace for milestones, uploads, previews, approvals, notes, and launch tracking so the project does not get buried in email.

ongoing.q: What if I need ongoing changes later?
ongoing.a: That can be a follow-on project or a support arrangement, depending on the kind of work. We do not trap deliverables behind a maintenance hostage model.

fit.q: What if my problem is not the website?
fit.a: Then we will say so. Sometimes the real issue is checkout friction, bad ops, or manual internal routing, and that should change the lane we recommend.

contact.metaDescription: Reach the studio for proposals, support, legal notices, and portal questions.
contact.title: Contact & Support
contact.description: For proposals, support, legal notices, and portal issues, use the details below.

webApp.title: Tell us about your custom web app
webApp.description: Quick context — what problem are you solving, who would use it, and when do you need it live? Email us and we'll route to a free 30-minute strategy call to scope properly.

portal.title: Tell us about the portal you need
portal.description: Who logs in (clients, staff, both), what they do there, and what's the current pain point? Email us and we'll set up a scoping conversation.

rescue.title: Tell us what's broken on your current site
rescue.description: URL, what isn't working, and what you've already tried. Email us and we'll do a quick triage and propose audit or sprint scope.

privacy.metaDescription: How CrecyStudio collects, uses, and protects information from clients and visitors.
privacy.label: Legal
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: We collect only data needed to quote, deliver, and support client projects. We do not sell data, run advertising, or share information with third parties beyond the sub-processors listed here.
privacy.collect1: Contact details — name, email address, company name, and phone number submitted via intake or contact forms.
privacy.collect2: Project details — goals, budget, timeline, and content submitted during the intake process.
privacy.collect3: Payment information — processed exclusively by Stripe. We never see or store card numbers.
privacy.collect4: Usage data — page views, referral source, and session duration collected in aggregate by analytics software.
privacy.collect5: IP addresses and technical metadata collected for security and fraud prevention.
privacy.use1: Prepare scoped quotes and project recommendations.
privacy.use2: Deliver project milestones and workspace updates.
privacy.use3: Send project-related communications. No marketing without explicit consent.
privacy.use4: Detect and prevent fraudulent submissions and abusive requests.
privacy.use5: Meet legal obligations such as tax records and dispute resolution documentation.
privacy.subprocessorsBody: Data is shared only with the vendors required to operate our services. Each is contractually bound to protect your data.
privacy.sp2: Supabase — database, authentication, and file storage (AWS, United States)
privacy.sp3: Vercel — hosting and content delivery (global edge)
privacy.retention1: Inquiry records: 2 years if no project is initiated.
privacy.retention2: Active project data: retained for the engagement period plus 1 year.
privacy.retention3: Payment records: 7 years to meet tax and legal requirements.
privacy.retention4: You may request deletion at any time. We will delete what we are not legally required to retain.
privacy.cookiesBody: We use analytics cookies to understand aggregate site traffic. We do not use advertising cookies or cross-site tracking. You can disable cookies in your browser settings without affecting site functionality.
privacy.rightsBody: If you are in the EU or UK, you have the right to access, correct, port, or delete your personal data, and to object to or restrict processing. To exercise any right, email
privacy.ccpaBody: We do not sell or share personal information as defined under the CCPA. California residents may request disclosure of the categories of data we hold or ask for deletion by emailing hello@crecystudio.com.

terms.metaDescription: Terms governing CrecyStudio services. Signed proposals control scope and pricing where they are more specific.
terms.label: Legal
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Using CrecyStudio services means you agree to these terms. Signed proposals control scope, pricing, and deliverables whenever they are more specific than this page.
terms.scopeBody: All quotes are estimates until approved in writing. Work does not begin until a written agreement is in place. Requests outside the agreed scope require a signed change order with revised pricing and timeline before any additional work begins.
terms.payments1: A deposit (typically 30–50% of the project total) is required to reserve your project slot.
terms.payments2: Remaining balances are invoiced per the milestone schedule in the proposal. Work does not advance past a milestone until that payment clears.
terms.payments3: Invoices unpaid for more than 7 calendar days may pause project work. Invoices unpaid for more than 30 days may result in cancellation, with work completed to date billed at pro-rata rates.
terms.payments4: Prices in proposals are valid for 30 days from the date issued.
terms.payments5: Invoices unpaid for more than 7 calendar days accrue interest at 1.5% per month (or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, whichever is lower) until paid in full.
terms.ownershipBody: All rights to custom deliverables transfer to you in full once the final invoice is paid. No rights are granted — not even a license to use the work — until payment is received in full. Internal frameworks, reusable utilities, templates, and operating methods developed by CrecyStudio remain its property. CrecyStudio retains the right to show completed work in its portfolio unless agreed otherwise in writing.
terms.indemnificationBody: You warrant that all content, materials, trademarks, data, and instructions you provide to CrecyStudio are lawfully yours to use and share, and do not infringe any third-party rights. You agree to indemnify and hold CrecyStudio harmless from any claim, loss, or expense — including reasonable legal fees — arising from your breach of this warranty or from your use or misuse of the deliverables.
terms.timeline1: Timelines begin when the deposit clears and all required input — content, credentials, brand assets — has been received.
terms.timeline2: If required input is not delivered within 14 calendar days of request, the timeline pauses until it arrives.
terms.timeline3: If a client is unresponsive for 30 or more consecutive calendar days, the project may be treated as abandoned. Deposits and milestone payments already made are forfeited. Restarting requires a new agreement and deposit.
terms.revisionsBody: Each engagement includes the number of revision rounds stated in the proposal. Additional revisions beyond that are billed at the current hourly rate. Revision requests submitted after written sign-off on a deliverable are treated as new scope, not corrections.
terms.nonDisparBody: Both parties agree not to make false, misleading, or materially harmful public statements about the other. This does not prevent either party from making accurate factual statements or from pursuing legitimate legal remedies.
terms.chargeback1: If you have a concern about a charge, email hello@crecystudio.com before initiating any dispute with your bank or card provider.
terms.chargeback2: All project communications, delivery records, and acceptance events are retained and may be submitted to payment processors to contest fraudulent claims.
terms.chargeback3: Initiating a chargeback for work that was delivered as agreed is a material breach of this contract and may result in the project being suspended and legal action to recover the amount plus collection fees.
terms.cancellationBody: If you cancel a project, all work completed to date is billable at pro-rata rates based on the agreed total. If you have already paid more than the pro-rata work warrants, the difference is refunded. If CrecyStudio cancels for cause — non-payment, abusive conduct — the same pro-rata billing applies with no further work obligations on either side.
terms.rightToCureBody: Before either party may claim a breach of this agreement, the non-breaching party must deliver written notice identifying the specific breach. The breaching party then has 14 calendar days to cure it. If cured within that window, no breach shall be deemed to have occurred for purposes of termination or liability.
terms.forceMajeureBody: Neither party is in breach for delays or failures caused by events outside their reasonable control — including illness, natural disasters, power outages, internet infrastructure failures, or third-party platform outages. The affected party must notify the other promptly. Timelines are extended by the duration of the disruption.
terms.liabilityBody: CrecyStudio's total liability for any claim is limited to the amount paid for the specific project giving rise to the claim. In no event is CrecyStudio liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, or special damages. All services are provided as-is; warranties beyond those stated in the signed proposal are disclaimed.
terms.lawBody: These terms are governed by the laws of the United States. Disputes that cannot be resolved directly will be submitted to binding arbitration under AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules. Either party may seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
terms.entireAgreementBody: This agreement and the signed proposal constitute the entire agreement between the parties and supersede all prior communications, representations, or understandings — written or verbal. If any provision is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions stay in full force. Failure to enforce any provision on a given occasion does not waive the right to enforce it in the future.
terms.supportBody: Email hello@crecystudio.com before initiating any billing dispute. Most issues are faster to resolve directly — a card dispute adds processing time for both parties and may not produce a better outcome.
terms.questionsBody3: for related information about data handling.

refund.metaDescription: CrecyStudio's refund policy for custom web development services — deposits, milestone payments, cancellations, and disputes.
refund.label: Legal
refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.intro: Our work is bespoke. When a project starts, time that could go to other clients is committed to you. This policy is designed to be fair while reflecting that reality.
refund.depositsBody: All deposits are non-refundable. The deposit reserves your slot in the schedule and covers discovery, scoping, and planning work — which happens whether or not the project proceeds. Exception: if CrecyStudio cancels before any work has begun, the deposit is refunded in full.
refund.milestonesBody: Once a milestone has been delivered and accepted, that payment is non-refundable. Acceptance is defined as written confirmation, payment of the next milestone invoice, or 14 calendar days without a written objection after delivery.
refund.cancellation1: If you cancel after work has begun, you owe for all work completed to date, calculated pro-rata against the agreed total price.
refund.cancellation2: If you have already paid more than the pro-rata work completed warrants, the difference is refunded.
refund.cancellation3: Cancellation requests must be submitted in writing to hello@crecystudio.com.
refund.thirdPartyBody: Fees paid to third parties on your behalf — domain registrations, hosting, fonts, stock assets, API credits, or software licenses — are non-refundable regardless of project status.
refund.never2: Discovery calls, scoping sessions, and written estimates.
refund.never3: Time spent waiting on client-side delays — missing content, credentials, or approvals.
refund.never4: Rush fees or priority scheduling surcharges.
refund.disputesBody: If you believe something was delivered incorrectly, email hello@crecystudio.com within 14 calendar days of delivery. We respond within 3 business days. Initiating a payment dispute or chargeback before contacting us forfeits the good-faith resolution process.
refund.processBody: Email hello@crecystudio.com with your project reference and the charge in question. We respond within 3 business days. Approved refunds are processed within 10 business days to the original payment method.

security.metaDescription: How CrecyStudio protects client data, project files, and workspace access.
security.label: Security
security.title: Security Practices
security.intro: Client data and project files are held to a high standard of care. This page documents the infrastructure, access controls, and practices we maintain.
security.infra1: Hosted on Vercel with automatic HTTPS, DDoS protection, and global edge delivery.
security.infra2: Database, authentication, and file storage powered by Supabase on AWS (US-East region).
security.infra3: Row-level security (RLS) policies are enforced at the database layer — no query executes without permission validation.
security.encryption1: All data in transit is encrypted with TLS 1.2 or higher. HTTPS is enforced on every route.
security.encryption2: Data at rest is encrypted with AES-256 via AWS-managed keys.
security.encryption3: Passwords are never stored by CrecyStudio. Client workspace access uses signed token links, not passwords.
security.workspace1: Each workspace is accessible via a unique cryptographically signed token — no shared login credentials.
security.workspace2: Tokens expire after 30 days of inactivity and require re-authentication after expiry.
security.workspace3: Admin access runs on a completely separate authentication path from client workspaces.
security.isolation1: Each client's data is isolated by row-level security policies — no client can access another client's data.
security.isolation2: File storage uses per-project access controls. Only authenticated parties with a valid token for that project can access its files.
security.backups1: Daily automated database backups with 7-day retention.
security.backups2: Point-in-time recovery is available within the active retention window.
security.backups3: Backups are stored in the same encrypted AWS environment as live data.
security.subprocessorsNote: Data is shared only with the vendors required to operate our platform. Each is contractually bound to protect your data.
security.wontDo1: We don't sell, share, or license client data to any third party.
security.wontDo2: We don't subcontract development work. Komlan is the only person with access to client environments and source code.
security.wontDo3: We don't retain client data indefinitely. Projects are archived after 1 year and deleted after 3 years unless legally required otherwise.
security.wontDo4: We don't store payment card data. All payment processing is handled entirely by Stripe.
security.yourResp1: Keep your workspace link private. Do not forward it to anyone not part of your project team.
security.yourResp2: Use a private or incognito browser session when accessing your workspace on a shared device.
security.yourResp3: Report any suspicious activity immediately to hello@crecystudio.com.
security.yourResp4: Remove workspace access for team members who leave your organisation.
security.disclosureBody: To report a security concern, email hello@crecystudio.com with subject line "Security". We acknowledge all reports within 48 hours and aim to resolve confirmed vulnerabilities within 72 hours.

aup.metaDescription: What CrecyStudio will and won't build. Policy on prohibited projects, mid-project termination, and third-party liability.
aup.label: Legal
aup.title: Acceptable Use Policy
aup.intro: CrecyStudio builds websites, web applications, and automation systems for legitimate businesses. This policy defines the project categories we won't accept, regardless of budget or timeline.
aup.wontBuildHeading: Content and projects we won't build
aup.wontBuild1: Sites or systems designed to deceive users — fake review platforms, phishing pages, impersonation sites, or anything intended to defraud.
aup.wontBuild2: Spam infrastructure — bulk email senders, SMS blasters, or any system designed for unsolicited mass outreach.
aup.wontBuild3: Any content that sexualises minors, promotes trafficking, or facilitates exploitation of any kind.
aup.wontBuild4: Malware, exploit kits, surveillance tools, or software designed to access systems without authorisation.
aup.wontBuild5: Platforms facilitating illegal gambling, unlicensed financial services, or pyramid and Ponzi schemes.
aup.wontBuild6: Projects whose purpose is to evade law enforcement, launder money, or obscure the origin of funds.
aup.wontBuild7: Sites that reproduce, resell, or redistribute copyrighted content without a valid license.
aup.wontBuild8: Any project where the brief, client identity, or stated business cannot be independently verified.
aup.declines1: All custom projects require a discovery call or detailed intake — we do not start work on anonymous briefs.
aup.declines2: We verify that the stated business exists and that the contact is affiliated with it.
aup.declines3: We decline projects where payment comes from an entity unrelated to the stated client.
aup.declines4: We decline rush requests that bypass the scoping process — urgency without a brief is the most common pattern in fraudulent engagements.
aup.declines5: We reserve the right to decline any project at the discovery stage without explanation.
aup.midProjectBody: If we discover that a project is being used for a prohibited purpose — whether stated in the original brief or revealed mid-engagement — we will terminate the contract immediately. Work completed to date will be billed. No further deliverables will be released.
aup.liabilityBody: CrecyStudio builds to the brief provided by the client. The client is solely responsible for ensuring their use of the deliverables complies with all applicable laws and regulations. CrecyStudio is not liable for any harm caused by a client's use or misuse of delivered work.
aup.ourSystemsBody: Access to our client workspace, intake forms, and project portal is granted solely for legitimate project use. Attempts to probe, reverse-engineer, scrape, or abuse CrecyStudio's own systems will result in immediate access revocation and may be reported to relevant authorities.
aup.reportBody: If you believe a CrecyStudio deliverable is being misused, or if you have concerns about a project brief, email hello@crecystudio.com. We take all reports seriously and respond within 48 hours.

website.prompt: My website makes my business look smaller than it is.
website.impact: Most clients judge trust in seconds. We redesign your first impression to convert more qualified leads.

ops.prompt: My team is stuck in repetitive manual handoffs.
ops.impact: We map bottlenecks, automate routing, and create cleaner operational systems that save hours each week.

ecom.prompt: Our checkout and store flow are leaking revenue.
ecom.impact: We diagnose conversion friction and fix the purchase journey from product pages to fulfillment.

starter.title: Starter Site
starter.body: For businesses needing a high-trust first impression quickly with focused conversion flow.

growth.title: Growth Site
growth.body: Adds deeper structure, integrations, and message clarity for scaling lead quality and volume.

premium.title: Premium Build
premium.body: Complex scope with custom interactions, advanced integrations, and multi-page strategic architecture.

step1.label: Intake
step1.detail: You submit scope details and priorities. We translate that into a clear execution path.

step2.label: Estimate
step2.detail: You receive a scoped range with timeline, deliverables, and recommendation within 24 hours.

step3.label: Workspace
step3.detail: Track progress, upload assets, approve milestones, and keep every decision in one place.

step4.label: Launch
step4.detail: Final QA, handoff, and go-live support so the finished system performs from day one.

founderCallout.name: Komlan Kouhiko — Founder & Sole Practitioner
founderCallout.body: Every project at CrecyStudio comes through me — not a project manager, not a junior. I've shipped SaaS platforms, built internal tools for real businesses, and run operations myself. You can reach me directly at any stage of the project.

websites.metaDescription: Premium website building for service businesses that need stronger trust, clearer messaging, and better lead conversion.

carePlans.metaDescription: Three monthly care plans that keep your site or custom system healthy — content updates, monitoring, small features, and improvement sprints.

stories.live: Your website is live and serving visitors. You can still submit feedback or upload new content at any time.
stories.launch_ready: Everything is in place. We're preparing your site for launch — you'll be notified as soon as it goes live.
stories.preview_review: We've published a preview of your website. Take your time looking through every page — when you're ready, leave feedback below or open the preview to see it live.
stories.build: We're building your website right now. You can track progress here, upload content, and submit feedback as things take shape.
stories.kickoff: Your deposit has been received and the project is queued for kickoff. We'll start building shortly.
stories.deposit_sent: We received your deposit notice and are verifying the payment. Work begins as soon as it clears.
stories.assets_submitted: Thanks for submitting your content. We're reviewing your assets and preparing to start the build.
stories.pre_start: Your project has been scoped. Review the agreement and deposit details below to get things moving.
stories.intake: Your project workspace is ready. Review the details below, upload your content, and we'll take it from there.

deposit.bodyPaid: Your deposit has been recorded and the project is moving through kickoff. We will keep the workspace updated as build work progresses.
deposit.bodyPending: Work begins as soon as the deposit clears. Pay securely via the link and we will confirm receipt within 24 hours.

directionModule.lockedNoticeDesignDirection: Design direction is locked. Feedback should focus on content accuracy, clarity, missing information, bugs, and reasonable polish. Major visual changes may affect the timeline or require a change order.

default.headline: getting set up

work.metaDescription: Selected systems we've shipped — including our own. Multi-tenant rental SaaS, AI-augmented service-contractor platform, and client work.
work.title: Real systems we've shipped — including our own.
work.subtitle: Two studio ventures we built, run, and operate. Plus client work where the names are sometimes anonymized.
work.venturesIntro: These are our own products, built and operated by the studio. They prove the engineering capability for the Web Apps lane — they're not client testimonials.

korent.name: Korent
korent.tagline: Management platform for inflatable rental businesses
korent.summary: Multi-tenant SaaS for inflatable rental businesses with three surfaces — public storefront, operator dashboard, and crew mobile workspace. Postgres with Row-Level Security for tenant isolation, delivery-route planning, document generation for rental agreements, and an AI operator copilot.

proveo.name: Proveo
proveo.tagline: Photo proof + CRM for service contractors
proveo.summary: SaaS for auto detailers, painters, and cleaners. AI photo detection identifying before/after pairs, freemium billing tiers, CRM pipeline, appointment scheduling, automated lifecycle and review-request emails.

work.clientIntro: Client case studies will land here as engagements complete. Anonymized first, then replaced with the named version when the client agrees.
work.comingSoon: First case study lands here soon

customAppIntake.metaDescription: Tell us what you're building. We'll scope the right engagement — discovery sprint, MVP, or full system — and schedule a call to walk through the details.
customAppIntake.title: Tell us about your project.

step1.heading: What are you building?
step1.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. A portal for our field technicians to log jobs, upload photos, and get paid without calling the office.
step1.descriptionHint: Give us a bit more detail — at least a sentence or two.

step2.heading: A bit more context.
step2.currentSolutionLabel: How do you handle this today?
step2.currentSolutionPlaceholder: e.g. We use a shared Google Sheet and text messages. It breaks down constantly.
step2.userScaleLabel: How many users do you expect?
step2.integrationsLabel: Which systems does this app need to connect to?
step2.integrationNotesLabel: For each integration above, describe whether data flows in, out, or both directions.
step2.integrationNotesPlaceholder: e.g. Salesforce read-only for contact sync, Stripe bidirectional for payments...
step2.complianceLabel: Do any of the following apply to your project?

step3.heading: Scope & timeline.
step3.stageLabel: Where are you in the process?
step3.scopeLabel: What kind of engagement are you looking for?
step3.timelineLabel: When do you need to be live?
step3.budgetLabel: What investment range have you set aside?
step3.budgetNote: Custom apps at CrecyStudio typically start at $15,000.
step3.decisionMakerLabel: Who else needs to be involved in approving this project?
step3.decisionMakerHelper: The day-to-day contact, budget approver, and technical sign-off are often different people — totally normal.
step3.decisionMakerPlaceholder: e.g. Me — I decide · Me + CTO for tech + CFO for budget
step3.approvalLabel: What needs to happen internally before you're ready to sign a contract?

step4.nextStepDetail: We'll review your intake, prepare questions, and walk through scope, timeline, and the right starting point together.
step4.contactHeading: Where should we send the confirmation?
step4.postLaunchLabel: After launch, how should hosting, maintenance, and updates be handled?
step4.budgetFlexibilityLabel: If the right solution costs 20–30% more than the range above, are you open to a conversation about it?
step4.budgetFlexibilityPlaceholder: e.g. Fixed — this is board-approved · Flexible if the business case is clear
step4.privacyNote: We'll only use your contact info to follow up on this project. No newsletters, no spam.

validation.validEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
validation.submitError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

exploring.label: Exploring an idea
exploring.desc: I haven't fully scoped this yet — I need help thinking it through.

validated.label: Validated, ready to build
validated.desc: I know what I want. I need someone who can actually build it.

funded.label: Funded and moving fast
funded.desc: Budget is approved. Timeline is the primary constraint.

replacing.label: Replacing an existing system
replacing.desc: I have something in production that needs to be rebuilt or extended.

discovery.label: Discovery sprint ($2,500 – $5,000)
discovery.desc: Get a scoped architecture, data model, and budget band before committing to a full build.

mvp.label: MVP build ($18K – $35K)
mvp.desc: Core features, production-grade, ready for real users.

full.label: Full system ($35K – $75K+)
full.desc: Multi-role, integrations, advanced features — the complete build.

not-sure.label: Not sure yet

step1.heading: Tell us about your business.
step1.userCountLabel: How many people will use this portal at launch?
step1.techTeamLabel: Do you have an internal developer or IT team involved?
step1.currentProcessLabel: How do you handle this today?
step1.currentProcessPlaceholder: e.g. We email PDFs and use a shared Google Drive.

step2.heading: What does it need to do?
step2.integrationsLabel: Which systems does this portal need to connect to?
step2.integrationNotesLabel: For the integrations above, describe what data needs to flow in, out, or both directions.
step2.integrationNotesPlaceholder: e.g. CRM data read-only, billing sync bidirectional...
step2.complianceLabel: Do any of the following apply to your project?

step3.heading: Project Context
step3.budgetLabel: What investment range have you set aside?

step3.budgetFlexibilityLabel: Is that range fixed, or is there flexibility depending on what the full solution requires?
step3.budgetFlexibilityPlaceholder: e.g. "Fixed — board-approved" or "Flexible if the ROI case is clear"
step3.deadlineLabel: Is there a specific date this needs to be live?
step3.deadlineReasonLabel: What business event drives that date?
step3.deadlineReasonPlaceholder: e.g. client onboarding starts Q3, contract renewal with current vendor...
step3.decisionMakerLabel: Who else in your organisation will be involved in reviewing or approving this project?
step3.decisionMakerHelper: The day-to-day contact, budget approver, and creative sign-off are often three different people — that's normal.
step3.decisionMakerPlaceholder: e.g. "Me — I decide" or "Me + CFO for budget + CTO for technical sign-off"
step3.approvalLabel: What does your approval process look like before signing?

step4.heading: Where should we send the scope?

rescueIntake.metaDescription: Tell us what's broken. We'll audit your current site and give you a clear fix plan with honest pricing.
rescueIntake.title: Tell us what's broken.

step1.heading: Tell us about your current site.
step1.platformLabel: What platform is it built on?
step1.builtWhenLabel: When was this site last built or redesigned?
step1.builtByPlaceholder: e.g. a freelancer, an agency, we did it in-house
step1.previousVendorLabel: Is your previous developer or agency still involved?

step2.heading: What's going wrong?
step2.alreadyTriedLabel: What has already been tried to fix this site?
step2.alreadyTriedPlaceholder: e.g. a developer tried to speed it up last month but it's still slow. Knowing what's been tried helps us avoid duplicating work.

step3.heading: Access & History
step3.assetAccessLabel: What do you currently have access to?
step3.assetAccessHelper: Check everything that applies. We'll ask about gaps below.
step3.assetAccessNotesLabel: For any items above where access is missing or unclear, describe what you know about where those accounts are held.
step3.assetAccessNotesPlaceholder: e.g. I think my old agency has the hosting login...
step3.previousVendorReasonLabel: What's prompting you to look for a new partner now?
step3.previousVendorReasonPlaceholder: e.g. the previous agency went dark, the project was abandoned, we had a dispute over access...
step3.seoLabel: Does your current site have Google rankings or organic traffic you want to preserve?
step3.urlsToPreserveLabel: Are there specific page URLs that must not change?
step3.urlsToPreservePlaceholder: e.g. /contact, /pricing — pages linked from print materials, bookmarked by customers, or heavily linked externally

step4.heading: Where should we send the audit?
step4.estimateNote: Rescue audits start at $800. Full fix scopes typically run $1,500 – $4,000 depending on what we find.
step4.budgetLabel: What investment range have you set aside?
step4.decisionMakerLabel: Who makes the final call on approving and funding this project?
step4.decisionMakerPlaceholder: e.g. Me — I'm the owner · My business partner and I · Marketing director with CFO approval
step4.notesPlaceholder: e.g. We relaunched 3 months ago and traffic dropped 40%. Agency ghosted us.
step4.privacyNote: We'll only use your contact info to follow up on this project. No newsletters, no spam.

validation.validEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
validation.submitError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

issues.slow: Site is slow — takes more than 3 seconds to load
issues.conversion: Poor conversion — visitors aren't becoming leads
issues.seo: SEO problems — traffic dropped or never worked
issues.cms: Can't update content without breaking things

critical.label: Critical — costing me customers right now
critical.desc: Every day it's broken is a lost sale.

soon.label: Important — needs fixing soon
soon.desc: Not on fire, but I want it resolved in the next few weeks.

low.label: Low — I want it right, not rushed

active.label: Yes — still working together
active.desc: They still have active access and we're working with them

ended-revoked.label: Ended — access revoked
ended-revoked.desc: The relationship is over and I've removed their access

ended-may-still-have.label: Ended — access unclear
ended-may-still-have.desc: The relationship ended but they may still have access

self-built.label: Self-built / no previous vendor
self-built.desc: We built it in-house or there's no previous vendor

not-sure.label: Not sure
not-sure.desc: I'm not certain what access they have

assetAccess.domain-registrar: Domain name (registrar login — GoDaddy, Namecheap, etc.)
assetAccess.cms-admin: CMS admin login (WordPress dashboard, Webflow Editor, etc.)
assetAccess.site-backup: Recent backup of site files and database

seo.yes-critical: Yes — rankings matter and must be preserved
seo.somewhat: Somewhat — some pages matter, others don't
seo.no: No — SEO is not a priority
seo.dont-know: I don't know my current SEO standing
